mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms synchronized consumer demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth months yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annualized during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints that compress corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of late-cycle months with INDPRO growth > 2%, or regime-conditional alpha is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 200,000 confirm robust labor demand that supports corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with payroll gains > 200k, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL index exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending relative to aggregate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP-retail divergence months yield positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during post-shock recovery phases signals strong capacity utilization that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of recovery-phase months with rising INDPRO, or regime-specific alpha is statistically insignificant.
